claim: MCP prompts are a more powerful primitive than tools alone; they enable agentic workflows by composing multiple tools, providing next-step guidance, and priming agent context automatically.
actionable: When building MCP servers, prioritize creating reusable prompts over individual tools; each prompt should orchestrate a complete workflow and offer suggestions for the next step.
source_title: How I build Agentic MCP Servers for Claude Code (Prompts CHANGE Everything)

claim: MCP servers expose a three-tier capability structure: Resources (lowest), Tools (middle), Prompts (highest). Most engineers skip Resources, overuse Tools, and completely miss the value of Prompts.
actionable: Restructure MCP server design: focus 60% effort on prompts, 30% on tools, 10% on resources. Test each prompt independently before integrating tools.
source_title: How I build Agentic MCP Servers for Claude Code (Prompts CHANGE Everything)

claim: MCP prompts can return rich structured information to agents and suggest next steps dynamically, guiding engineers toward the right domain-specific solutions without external documentation.
actionable: Design each MCP prompt to end with a bulleted list of suggested next steps the agent can take; this keeps engineers in-context and reduces context-switching.
source_title: How I build Agentic MCP Servers for Claude Code (Prompts CHANGE Everything)

claim: Organizing MCP prompts in isolated single-function Python files makes them testable, discoverable, and easily parallelizable across multiple agent instances.
actionable: Create one Python file per prompt; return a single string/structured output; store all files in a dedicated /prompts directory with kebab-case naming.
source_title: How I build Agentic MCP Servers for Claude Code (Prompts CHANGE Everything)

claim: Short, keyword-based slash commands (e.g., '/find-data-sources') in MCP prompts enable agents to invoke complex workflows with minimal context if the prompt has properly primed the knowledge base.
actionable: Design slash commands for your MCP prompts; combine them with context-priming prompts so agents can chain commands with just 2-3 keywords.
source_title: How I build Agentic MCP Servers for Claude Code (Prompts CHANGE Everything)

claim: MCP prompts that handle arbitrary code execution (like running Python or SQL) are more powerful than tools when they compose multiple tool calls and return structured results.
actionable: For data analysis MCP servers, create prompts that run arbitrary Python code instead of exposing individual analysis tools; let the prompt decide which tools to invoke.
source_title: How I build Agentic MCP Servers for Claude Code (Prompts CHANGE Everything)

claim: Cloud Code (running on Claude Sonnet 4) is the recommended LLM for MCP server development because it handles multi-file code inspection and refactoring better than other models.
actionable: When building MCP servers, use Claude Code with Sonnet 4 for testing and iterating on your MCP server implementation; it provides the best file navigation.
source_title: How I build Agentic MCP Servers for Claude Code (Prompts CHANGE Everything)

claim: Listing all MCP server capabilities (tools, resources, prompts) in a single discovery prompt makes the entire MCP server immediately usable without reading documentation.
actionable: Create a '/list-capabilities' or '/help' prompt that returns a formatted breakdown of all available tools, resources, and sub-prompts with brief descriptions.
source_title: How I build Agentic MCP Servers for Claude Code (Prompts CHANGE Everything)
